'''Crimler Naval Base''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] installation in [[Montrol City]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. Following his [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|defeat]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] that resulted in the loss of his capital, [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], stopped at Gandle Ott as he retreated with his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]]. Sarne's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] took on supplies from the base during his layover before continuing on into the depths of the sector.

==Description==
Crimler Naval Base was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] installation in [[Montrol City]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]], located in the [[Ott system]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. Comprised of the main [[Navy/Legends|naval]] base and several adjacent depots, the installation was stocked with supplies to re-provision [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]] assets.<ref name="DIRP" />

==Occupants==
The naval base was staffed by Imperial personnel who were loyal to [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], who ruled the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]<ref name="DIRP" /> after breaking away from the Empire following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] [[4 ABY/Legends|four years previously]].<ref name="TSB">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref>

==History==
Crimler Naval Base was established on the planet Gandle Ott in the Kathol sector of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. In 8 ABY, the base was under the control of [[Moff Sarne's forces|Imperial forces]] loyal to Moff Kentor Sarne, who ruled the sector as a warlord. Sarne, who had recently been [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] from his capital on the planet [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]], led the majority of his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to Gandle Ott as he retreated into the depths of the Kathol sector. While in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], he began loading supplies from Crimler Naval Base and recalled all reserve military personnel on the planet to duty. News of Sarne's defeat at Kal'Shebbol had not reached the planet, and the Moff claimed to the media that he was leading the large fleet on an extended mission to exterminate [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] throughout the sector.<ref name="DIRP" />

The Moff's actions were documented by [[Dana Cadwell]], a [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] for the [[NewsNet]] [[KatholNet]], who filed a news report entitled ''Moff Sarne Prepares For Pirate Purge''. A couple of [[Time/Legends|days]] later, the fleet departed from Gandle Ott to carry out the mission on which Sarne had claimed to be. In the aftermath of the departure, it was discovered that Sarne had not only taken the supplies but the majority of the planet's defense force and active military personnel with him, including the planetary [[Governor/Legends|governor]]. The loss of many high-ranking officials left Gandle Ott's government in disarray, leading to [[Politics/Legends|political]] infighting between senior government figures Vice-Governor [[Marja Lang]] and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Herron Dade]] over who should take over the absent governor's position.<ref name="DIRP" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Crimler Naval Base was mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|RPG]] adventure ''[[Death Is Remotely Possible]]'', which was published in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]]. It played no part in the adventure and was only mentioned in an [[in-universe]] NewsNet article.<ref name="DIRP" />
